passages.name: EvansDepartmentStoreChristinaDialogue0
passages.content:
Christina glares at you as you approach. An eyebrow lifts, almost daring you to speak. In spite of the hostile invitation, you decide to leave her alone.

 

 **Actions**

 <> 

 Christina looks up as you approach. “Oh hey, Em. Doing a little shopping?”

 

 You nod. “Something like that.”

 

 The cheerleader rolls her eyes just a little, but pulls out a white leather miniskirt from the clothing rack. “What do you think?” she asks you. “Sophisticated or slutty? I can’t decide.”

 

 You shrug, unsure what Emily would say, or what the right answer is. Christina laughs at your reaction.

 

 “Really, Em. We need to work on your sense of style.” Christina eyes you, appraisingly. “How would you like to do some retail therapy? I’m sure Daddy wouldn’t mind if we picked out some things for you. So. My treat.”

 

 You look back at Christina. Do you want to spend your time with her?

 

 **Actions**

 <>

 <>

 Christina politely smiles as you approach. “Oh hi there,” she says, the sing-song tones in her voice just a little too forced.

 

 “Hey Christina,” you reply. “What are you doing?”

 

 “Browsing the new ranges,” she tells you. “My father might be the businessman of our house, but he has terrible taste. Mum picks out most of the stock.”

 

 She eyes you with her cold blue eyes, but turns her attention back to the clothing racks. “I’ll see you at school, then,” she adds, not looking back at you.

 

 Understanding an implied dismissal when you’ve received one, you say goodbye and step away, leaving her to shop.

 

 **Actions**

 <> 

 Christina looks up from the clothing racks as you approach. Her icy eyes look over you, a smile forming. “Jennifer,” she says. “Why aren’t you working? Or have you been slacking off again in the storeroom?”

 

 You glare back at her, trying to hold your head high. At this, she titters. “Oh, Jen. Let’s not pretend. It’s been years since you’ve frightened me. Don’t pretend that we don’t know who you are, now.”

 

 The resolve within

passages.name: EvansDepartmentStoreChangingRoom
passages.content:
Christina motions toward the row of curtained-off changing cubicles, standing just outside with her arms crossed. “Go on, Em,” she instructs, tilting her head toward the nearest one.

 

 You hesitate for only a moment before stepping inside, pulling the curtain closed behind you. A breath of relief escapes as you realize Christina isn’t following. Even as Emily, the thought of undressing in front of her - especially here, in public - would be too much.

 

 Still, the reprieve is short-lived. The curtain rustles, and a sliver of Christina’s face appears at the side, peeking in. “Strip to your underwear,” she orders casually, as if it’s the most natural request in the world.

 

 You blink, caught off guard. “What?”

 

 Christina sighs, rolling her eyes. “Em, I have to see what we’re working with. Clothes should complement your natural shape. Now hurry up.”

 

 There’s no real room to argue. Exhaling slowly, you begin undressing, sliding out of your clothes until you’re left in just your underwear. The room suddenly feels cooler against your skin.

 

 When you mutter, “Done,” Christina doesn’t wait. With a swift motion, she pulls back the curtain, exposing you completely to her scrutinizing gaze. Her eyes flick up and down, appraising, as if she’s evaluating a dress on display. You shift uncomfortably under the intensity of it.

 

 “Like I thought,” she says at last, arms still folded. “You have some natural assets we can work with.”

 

 Before you can even process that, the door to the changing area opens.

 

 Jennifer steps in, holding a neat stack of clothes, and immediately freezes. Her eyes widen at the sight of you in just your underwear. “Oh,” she stammers, quickly averting her gaze. “Sorry. I didn’t mean to --”

 

 Christina dismisses her embarrassment with a wave. “Oh, don’t be such a prude, Jennifer. Just do your job and make sure no one else comes in here while we work. Understand?”

 

 Jennifer looks up at her, grimacing as she bites her tongue.

passages.name: EvansDepartmentStoreJenniferWork
passages.content:
**Evans' Department Store - Staff Room**

 [image: ./images/Evans_Department_Store_Staff_Room.png]

 

 Your heart thrums with a dull mix of dread and resignation as you push open the “Staff Only” door, stepping into the dimly lit back halls of Evans’. You walk quickly and quietly, certain that somebody is going to call you out for entering a private area, not open to the public.

 

 With some trepidation you move through the endless corridors. You pass a cleaner, polishing the floor. A lone electrician, fiddling with a fuse box. Two other sales assistants that you don’t recognise. 

 

 No one treats you as though you shouldn’t be there. Nobody stops you. Nobody questions you. Instead, they barely acknowledge you.

 

 In the cramped staff room, the bulletin board groans under the weight of today’s roster. Names scrawled in bright marker mock you:
 
 

 “Stock control - Jenna! The early zest gets the dress!” 
 

 A sigh escapes you. In the grand scheme of retail, inventory is the dullest rung on the ladder.

 

 You punch the time clock and steel yourself for a morning of dusty boxes and misplaced price tags. Does Jennifer really do this every day?
 
 
 **Evans' Department Store - Staff Room**

 [image: ./images/Evans_Department_Store_Staff_Room.png]

 

 You push open the Staff Only door and walk the back corridors of the department store, this time without the anxiety of being an imposter. In some ways the reality is worse. Despite working in the same store, people go out of their way to avoid acknowledging you.

 

 Entering the staff room, you check the schedule. Once again, you’ve been assigned to the stock room in the morning. You take a breath, then turn on your heels and walk out.

passages.name: EvansDepartmentStoreJenniferWorkResponses
passages.content:
“You see,” he begins, and you instantly know there’s a wave of bullshit coming your way. “The loading bay has been blocked by someone’s new car. These rich folk, think they own this town, don’t they? I can’t get my van in there.” He looks down at your body once again. “It’s too tight to fit in.”

 

 You fold your arms. “Then park on the double yellows and deliver,” you fire back.

 

 Martin’s lips twitch into a grin. He steps forward and you find yourself backing up, brushing against the unopened crate next to the door. “And risk a fine?” he says. “Fifty quid for a few boxes of ladies’ underwear? Not worth it.” His voice dips low, rough as gravel. “Council don’t care if I’m late, but they love writing tickets.”

 

 You let out a huff. “That’s not my problem. You’re paid to deliver.”

 

 He straightens, expression twisting into something almost like pity. “Funny,” he says, voice thick with mock regret, “I heard about you.” He leans in, boots scraping the concrete as he shifts his weight. “Big fall from grace, isn’t it?”

 

 His hand drifts to the crate behind you, pressing just enough to pin you there. “Makes me wonder,” he murmurs, eyes dark. “How far you’ll go to stay on top… even just in this shop?”

 

 **Actions**

 
 
 [The Appeaser] <>

 
 
 
 <>

 <>
 
 
 
 
 
 [The Appeaser] <> - You can't seem to stand up for yourself

 
 <>

 <>
 
 
 
 You can’t help it. Before you even think, the words spill out of your mouth. “Alright, Martin. What is it you want?”

 

 His grin turns predatory. “Something I’ve been hoping for all morning. Just a kiss.” 

 

 He looks at you. You feel like you don’t have a choice. Not with him. You can only nod.

 

 Before you can react, he sweeps you up on the crate. Your skirt rides up as he settles you down, his eyes now on the same level as yours. He pushes forward, parting your legs, skirt now up around your waist.

 

 He leans in close, voice low. “Just a little one. I’m sure you’ll enjoy it.”

 

 His lips crash against you

passages.name: EvansDepartmentStoreReception
passages.content:
**Evans' Department Store**

[image: ./images/Evans_Department_Store_Reception.png]

A wood-paneled reception dominated by a lone desk, an old rotary telephone, and a frosted door with Terry Evans' name stenciled into it. The muted decor radiates sterile authority and quiet isolation.

 You slip away from the sales floor, heart thudding, and quickly ascend the narrow staircase to the upper level. The air grows still and hushed. Gone are the bright rails and glass displays, replaced by warm wood paneling, muted green accents, and quiet overhead lights that lend the reception a calm, somber precision.

 

 A lone wooden desk stands beneath a wall clock, an old rotary phone perched at its corner; framed abstract prints hang on the walls, their muted tones echoing the soft carpet underfoot.

 

 Behind the desk sits Mary Handle. A woman in her forties with motherly poise. Her blouse is crisply ironed, her hair pulled into a tidy chignon, and though laugh lines crease the corners of her eyes, she wears them like a badge of experience. When she looks up, her gaze is both welcoming and perceptive, as if she’s seen every kind of face pass through these doors.

 

 She looks up from the shift roster, offering you a warm, practised smile. “Jennifer,” she says, voice soft and steady. “Everything all right? Need to see Mr. Evans? He’s always happy to make time for his girls.” 

 

 You step forward, your heart ticking like the clock on the wall. You know Mary is Terrence’s mother and that the note you found at Jennifer’s apartment had her phone number on. You need to find out what it means, and you bet that Mary can help you make sense of it.
 
 
 Mary continues to patiently wait for you to talk. A soft creak from inside Terry’s office makes her jump. The pulse in your throat quickens.
 
 
 Mary waits for you to speak. Through the frosted door you hear heavy footsteps, followed by a growl of curses and muttered profanity that rattles the wood. Mary’s polite smile falters for th

passages.name: EvansDepartmentStoreSleepwear
passages.content:
Almost like a dream, you wander through the empty store, finding yourself quickly standing in the sleepwear section. Your mind races. Too often you've seen things in this section where your mind has jumped to what Jennifer would like wearing it. Now, the thought process is the same. But in the image, you’re Jennifer. And Jennifer is you.

 

 Do you want to appear sexy for your man? the snide voice that lives in your head snaps at you. Imagining what he’ll say when he sees you.

 

 You bite your lip. Your eye wanders to a rack of silk nightwear. You’ve seen Jen in a similar one before, but these seem like a different design. The hemline short, the top more open. Backless, tied together by lace trim.

 

 Something in you stirs. A picture of what it would be like to wear it. You can’t escape it. Looking at it is turning you on.

 

 You pick up a couple, and mull over what colour to buy.

 

 **Actions**

 <>

 <>

 <>

 
 You pick out the pink colour. A small giggle escapes as you run it through the till, feeling the silk material in your fingers. You picture yourself in it. Standing in Jennifer’s bedroom. Looking cute. Girly. Feminine.

 

 And shut down the memory as the thought of who is watching as you model it.

 

 **Actions**

 
 <>

 <>
 
 
 You pick out the red colour. A sultry laugh escapes as you run it through the till, feeling the silk material in your fingers. You picture yourself in it. Standing in Jennifer’s bedroom. Looking alluring. Sexy. Hot.

 

 And shut down the memory as the thought of who is watching as you model it.

 

 **Actions**

 
 <>

 <>

 
 You pick out the black colour. A wicked cackle escapes as you run it through the till, feeling the silk material in your fingers. You picture yourself in it. Standing in Jennifer’s bedroom. Looking assertive. Confident. Dominant.

 

 And shut down the memory as the thought of who is watching as you model it.

 

 **Actions**

 
 <>

 <>

passages.name: EvansDepartmentStoreShopWorkout
passages.content:
The male selection is sparse but straightforward. One rack of folded shorts and T-shirts in muted greys and dark blues. No labels boast of trend or appeal. Every one lists out the benefits to exercise. Moisture-wicking, stretch-fit, durable.

 

 You find a pair of shorts in your size, along with a workout shirt. The fabric is light but tough, designed to move with you and soak up sweat without complaint. There’s nothing flashy about any of it. No mesh panels, no logos, no tailoring to accentuate anything.

 

 It’s not meant to show you off. It’s meant to let you workout. As you check the fit, you feel readiness. These clothes are for testing yourself. Pushing yourself. Winning.

 

 Actions

 <>
 
 You look around Evans’ Department Store for any sign of workout clothes. After some searching, you notice a sign pointing to “Activewear.”

 

 The first thing you notice is how small everything is. Cropped tops, high-cut leggings, sheer mesh panels. The whole collection seems less designed for exercise and more for visibility. You scan the tags. Nothing here promises support or durability. But they all will bring attention.
 
 
You aren’t sure how you feel about wearing anything here, especially as Emily. Even if you only wear it whilst working out, people will still see. The thought of what you will look like as they watch unnerves you.

 

 After flicking through racks, you realise there are realistically only three options available to you. On-brand for Evans, the options are distinguished by colour - Red, Black and Pink - but more than the colours, the trims and design on each couldn’t be more different.

 

 The red set is made of material that is slick, tight, and unmistakably meant to be looked at. The top has a deep scoop neck, and the leggings cut high across the hips. Wearing this would make your body impossible to ignore. When you fold it over your arm, you can’t help but think about being seen in it. And why it feels exciting to imagine it.

 

 In contrast

passages.name: FreshPotTransformFlo
passages.content:
You feel the amulet tingle underneath your clothes and let your hand move to touch Flo’s paperback. *Here we go*, you think.

 

 Flo frowns at you as you stare at her. You look her up and down, taking in her flame-red hair, her ruby red lipstick and her plucked eyebrows. You form a picture of her in your mind, dressed in her waitress uniform, her warm smile beaming. 

 

 “You okay, ?” you hear her say. 

 

 “From this item, I see you in your time of need. Now form shape to the person I see within.”

 

 The amulet burns against your chest and you feel the sensation of magical energy leaping across your body. You scream as you feel the waves rippling out from the amulet. Your scalp feels like it’s on fire and your body starts to crunch as changes sweep across it.

 

 
 Now familiar sensations register inside your brain. Your chest changes. Arms morph. Bones inside your body crunching. Your womb burns as it shifts and changes.
 
 
 Now familiar sensations register inside your brain. Chest expanding. Arms shrinking. Bones inside your body crunching. Penis burning as it shrivels back inside your body.
 
 
 

 And with a final shriek, the pain subsides. You look up, seeing Florence staring back at you in abject horror. A strand of flame-red hair falls across your eye. 

 

 **Actions**

 <>

 You prepare for the change, but something feels off. The amulet's energy seems drained. 

 

 You curse inwardly, realising the amulet needs more time to re-charge.

 

 **Actions**

 <>

passages.name: FreshPotTransformFlo2
passages.content:
“Fall asleep,” you say quickly, “and let your memories drift as you slumber.”

 

 Florence stares at you for just a second, then drops behind the counter. You round the counter and see her passed out on the floor. Once you are satisfied she’s taken no injuries from her sudden fall, you look up in the mirrored glass behind the counter to see your reflection.

 

 The perfectly made-up face of Florence looks back at you. Her bushy flame-red hair. Her luscious red lips. Her finely plucked eyebrows.

 

 You become aware of the waitress uniform you are wearing. The hem of her skirt brushing against the bottom of your bum. Her blue blouse tight, hugging your curves. Breasts held firmly in place by a bra. The squeeze of her feet in the glossy red heels.

 

 You smile to yourself, feeling giddy. Your brain seems foggy and for a moment you completely forget why you are holding a paperback book. You feel your *intelligence* has been impacted by your most recent change.

 

 You take a moment more to acknowledge your new form before turning your attention to the pressing matter of the unconscious Florence at your feet.

 

 **Actions**

 <>

 
 You grab Florence with both your arms and drag her through the kitchen to the back room. The lack of strength in your new form is noticeable, whilst her heels are higher than you're used to. A few times you are perilously close to losing your balance. You shudder to think what would happen if you fell and were knocked out.

 
 You grab Florence with both your arms and drag her through the kitchen to the back room. The lack of strength in your new form is noticeable, as is moving backwards in high heels. A few times you are perilously close to losing your balance. You shudder to think what would happen if you fell and were knocked out.

 
 

 The back room is little bigger than a closet, barely able to contain the small desk covered in paperwork. With little choice, you prop Florence up in the desk chair, trying to make her as comfort

passages.name: StaceyBritneyFreshPot
passages.content:
You quietly slide into the booth behind Stacey and Britney in an attempt to listen to their conversation, but as soon as you sit down they fall silent and shoot you a look. It appears that if you want to listen into their conversation, you’ll need to be less conspicuous.

 

 **Actions**

 <>
 
 Britney and Stacey see you approach. Stacey is cautious, but Britney smiles. She welcomes you to sit down with them.

 

 **Actions**

 <>
 
 
 
 Stacey and Britney sit tucked into their usual corner booth, half-finished frappes sweating on the table. You move past them slowly, clearing cups, cloth in hand. They don’t notice. Or don’t care.

 

 “I swear he’s doing it on purpose,” Stacey mutters, arms folded tight. “He’s shutting down again.”

 

 Britney sighs, resting her chin on her palm. “He was doing so well. Then bam! Walls up. He’s not even looking at me anymore. And don’t say it’s nerves. I couldn't have been more flirty.”

 

 "How's that different to usual?” Stacey quips. Britney scowls at her.

 

 “Whatever," she replies. "Chrissie’s not happy. That's what matters. She said that if I couldn't bring a boy around, what value did I have?"

 

 Stacey grimaces. "It's got something to do with that book. I know it."

 

 "That biology one?" Britney bites her lip. "You think he’s choosing diagrams over me?"

 

 "No doofus," Stacey snaps. "I’ve seen him reading it during lunch. It's full of notes. He's been different since he's been reading it."

 
 
 Britney sighs. “So why don't we pick his locker? He doesn't take it everywhere.”

 

 "You know how to pick a locker?" Stacey says, raising her eyebrows.

 

 "People do it on TV all the time!" Britney bites back.

 

 "Well, if you manage to learn how to use a hairpin to pick a lock, let me know," Stacey says, rolling her eyes.

 

 "We could... ask that janitor for a key?" Britney offers.

 

 Stacey gives her a dark look. "I am not going back to that basement. Not after last time.”

 

 Britney exhales, frustrated. “We’

passages.name: FreshPotFloWork
passages.content:
The morning shift at Fresh Pot is quiet. The windows are still misted from the early air, and the soft rattle of the espresso machine hums beneath the low murmur of conversation. You glance around the café and sigh, stepping into Florence’s routine. Wiping down surfaces. Replacing bakery stock. Folding napkins just so. You try to mimic the easy efficiency you've seen from her on your rare visits.

 

 Then the door creaks open… and Andrea walks in. You almost don’t recognise her at first.

 

 Gone is the lacy French Maid uniform that Charlton Hotel insists on. Instead, she’s wearing a simple cardigan and jeans. Her hair tied back loosely. It's the first time you’ve seen her dressed out of costume. For a moment, that feels more disorienting than the uniform ever did.

 

 She gives you a glance - soft, tired - and heads straight for one of the booths near the back.

 

 You wipe your hands, glance toward the empty counter, and cross the floor. “Hey,” you say gently. “You alright?”

 

 Andrea shrugs. She tries to play it off with a small exhale through her nose, but the slump in her shoulders tells you everything. “Didn’t sleep,” she mutters. “Lennox kept me late. Had me redoing the same load of linen three times. Then he asked if I’d ‘ironed the bows’ on my apron before I left.”

 

 You raise an eyebrow. She gives a hollow smirk. “I didn’t know I was supposed to. Guess I’ll add that to the list.”

 

 You sit across from her as she stares at the table. “And Sermon... he’s been watching me more. Following me with his eyes.”

 

 There’s a pause. She doesn’t look up. “He used to do the same with Amber. Before she quit. And we all know where she ended up.”

 

 Your stomach twists. You’re not sure who Amber is, or where she ended up. But you bet it isn’t good.

 

 The bell over the door rings again. Customers. The line at the counter is starting to build. You know you won’t earn tips unless you’re behind it, smiling sweetly and handing over cappuccinos with perfect

passages.name: FreshPotWorkResponses
passages.content:
You glance back at the growing line of customers… then sit.

 

 Andrea doesn’t speak right away. She just lets out a breath and leans her head in her hand, fingers rubbing at her temple. “I know I need the job,” she murmurs eventually. “It’s not that I’m… ungrateful. It’s just…”

 
 
 She trails off, staring at the tabletop like it might have the answers.

 

 “I keep thinking about us. Back at school. You remember? Always thinking things would work out. The teachers were full-on. Sure. But I thought that was the worst of it. Turns out the real world’s just… slower to understand. But meaner. More humiliating. Is this really all there was waiting for us on the other side?”

 

 Your eyes flick towards the counter. The queue is creeping towards the door. A number of impatient customers give you dirty looks. But you turn back to the young woman opposite you. She exhales again, steadier now.

 

 “Sorry. Didn’t mean to dump all that on you. I just needed to sit for a second.”

 

 Before you can respond, a voice snaps from the counter. “What do you have to do to get a coffee around here?”

 

 Andrea rises, gathering her bag. “I should go. Thanks for listening, Flo.”

 

 She slips out the door. Shoulders still heavy, but maybe a little less.

 

 You turn, apologising with a smile as you move around the counter to serve the irritated customers. They barely acknowledge your efforts and tips are not forthcoming. But you feel that, at least, you’ve made Andrea’s day a little better.

 
 You sit beside her, trying to ignore the queue forming. She tells you a little more about her evening. About Lennox. And Pierre. On the surface, she is doing what you’d expect from a maid. Cleaning. Washing. Helping out in the kitchen. But the tone from the rest of the staff. You can’t imagine it is easy at all.

 

 A number of hisses and tuts come from the side, as customers increasingly look at you expecting services. Andrea smiles sweetly, but doesn’t talk much more. When she gets up,

passages.name: FreshPotWorkEvents
passages.content:
With a cursory glance around the cafe, you sigh and get to work. You spend your time preparing take-away food and wiping down counters, trying to mimic some of the things you’ve seen Flo do when you’ve swung by for coffee.

 

 
 Walking around the cafe, you adjust to working in such high heels. On more than one occasion you almost slip, but thankfully there’s always a table or countertop for you to grasp on to. It never ceases to make customers chuckle when you stumble. 

 
 Walking around the cafe, you slowly adjust to working in heels. On more than one occasion you almost slip, but thankfully there’s always a table or countertop for you to grasp on to. It never ceases to make customers chuckle when you stumble. 

 
 

 In fact, there’s a lot that you do that make customers react. Without exception everyone treats you as an old friend. You find yourself returning their smiles and laughing at their jokes. Especially when you receive tips for your efforts.

 

 It does not go unnoticed how men and women treat you differently. Women are polite and chatty, but the men in their different ways are flirtatious and voyeuristic. You feel their eyes on your legs as you saunter around the coffee shop. You see them staring at your breasts as you lean over to serve them. 

 

 One particularly flirty customer is Dan, from Thompson’s Repairs. You inwardly sigh when you see him walk in. He smiles like a tiger when he sees you and doesn’t take his eyes off you as you prepare a coffee for him.

 

 “Damn fine cup of coffee” he says as you bring a coffee to his booth. As he reaches into his wallet to pay, you catch him looking you up and down. “Although with legs like those, I’d drink anything you served me.”

 

 You notice he is taking his time rifling through notes, counting out how much he’s going to pay you.

 

 **Actions**

You're not sure how it could teach you how to be assertive, but you take note of the location - Casey's Gym, on Orchid Road.

passages.name: Gym
passages.content:
**Casey's Gym**

[image: ./images/Gym.png]

Basic by any standards, Casey’s Gym is no more than a room covered in soft mats and littered with exercise gear. A full length mirror runs down one side of the room and a ballet barre runs down the entire length.

Gordon, one of the local labourers, sits at one of the weight lifting machines. His tight biceps flex as he pulls the plates high into the air, and then slowly lowers them down with control and confidence.

 
 
Casey stands in the centre of the gym, kicking gym mats together in a square. His hands are encased in blue boxing gloves, yet somehow a lit roll-up dangles on his bottom lip.

 
Casey stands behind the till, leafing through a boxing magazine. A lit roll-up hangs on his lips, which he occasionally takes a drag on.

Mrs. DeBussy stands beside the full-length mirror, clipboard clasped firmly to her chest. She wears a collared polo shirt tucked into thick black leggings, ballet slippers barely visible beneath the hem.

Jake, the school quarterback, is at the back of the gym, running through some dumbbell exercises. Jake seems to strain at the weight he's chosen, regularly just letting go of the dumbbell once he's lifted it, letting it crash to the floor.

 
 
*You watch Jake for a moment. If you really wanted to know what went on in those boys’ classes, Jake could be an option. If you could get your hands on something of his, something personal. Something to power the amulet. Getting him somewhere private wouldn't be a challenge.*

 

**Actions**

<>

<>

<>

<>

[[Go outside->OrchidRoad]]

passages.name: GymJakeDialogue0
passages.content:
The jock’s eyes flick from the window to you, roaming your body. And yet he remains silent.

 

 “Hey Jake,” you say.

 

 “What do you want?” he spits. “Or did you decide you want to see what a real man looks like?”

 

 He stares back at you, almost daring you to say something. When you don’t reply, his eyes drift to over your shoulder. You glance back, noticing two girls watching from outside, whispering and giggling as they subtly eye Jake.

 
 
 
 The jock raises his eyebrow as you approach, his eyes flicking across your body.

 

 “Hey Jake,” you greet him.

 

 “Hey yourself, nerd,” he says, a wry smile resting on his face. His eyes drift over your shoulder for a moment, and when you glance behind you notice two girls watching from outside, whispering and giggling as they watch the jock.

 

 But when you turn back, Jake’s eyes are firmly settled on you.
 
 
 
 Jake's eyes drift up and down as you step near him. He gives you a smile that you assume he thinks is seductive. It comes across as predatory and lecherous.

 

 “Hey there,” he says to you. “Haven’t seen you around for a while.”

 

 You study him carefully, not sure of the relationship you currently have with him. “I’ve been busy,” you tell him. 

 

 He smiles, his eyes again lingering over your body. “What can I do for you, sweetheart?”

 

 Before you can reply, you hear muted girlish laughter from behind you. Turning, you see two girls watching through the window, whispering as they try to pretend they aren’t watching Jake.

 

 But when you turn back to him, you can’t help but notice his attention rests firmly with you.

 
 Jake looks at you with derision as you approach. “Yeah?” he snaps. “What are you looking at?”

 

 You are about to reply when you hear muted giggles from behind you. Turning, you note there are two girls looking into the gym from outside, whispering and eying both you and Jake with interest. 

 

 By the look of his lingering grin and drifting eyes, Jake has noticed too.

passages.name: GymDeBussyDialogue
passages.content:
As you approach, DeBussy looks up, her face lighting with a beaming smile. “Oh! Emily. What a delight. I didn’t expect to see you here on a weekend. But then, you are one of my more proactive girls.”

 

 Her eyes drop momentarily to her clipboard before returning to yours. “Here to take part in my ballet instruction, are you?” she asks, voice syrupy. “A sharpened mind requires a graceful body, after all. And discipline is discipline, no matter the subject.”

 

 Her smile lingers, polite and practiced. “I’d be very pleased to include you. Provided, of course, you’re willing to take it seriously.”

 
 As you approach, DeBussy looks up. A polite smile forms. “Hello dear. I didn’t expect to see you, today. What brings you here?”

 

 She straightens a little, folding her arms over the clipboard. Her ballet slippers peek out beneath her leggings, though she makes no effort to draw attention to them. “Are you here to partake in my ballet lessons?” she asks when you don’t respond. “Typically I don’t encourage my students to partake. Weekends are for reflection. Quiet study. Perhaps even a little revision.”

 

 Her eyes meet yours, as if burrowing into your brain to work out your thoughts. “For you though,” she says after some time. “I will make an exception.” A thin smile forms on her lips, but she stands waiting for your answer. 

 
 
 DeBussy looks up as you approach, a smile appearing that can’t quite mask her confusion. “Good afternoon,” she murmurs. “You’re Emily’s , aren't you? What brings you here?”

 
 
 You pause before replying, a cold shiver spreading up your spine. “Yes. I’m Emily’s .”

 

 
 At this, DeBussy breaks into a smile. “Oh! She’s a bright girl! I shouldn’t say this, but between us two, she’s one of my favourites. So diligent. Observant. A sharp eye for detail. She’ll go far, with the right tutoring.”

passages.name: GymGordonTrainFemale
passages.content:
**Casey's Gym**

[image: ./images/Gym.png]

 
 Gordon doesn’t look up as you step back onto the mats, but his voice acknowledges you. “Alright. Let’s begin.”

 

 He’s moved a bench into place near the wall-length mirror. A set of pink dumbbells sit neatly arranged beside it. But even as you scan the equipment, you catch your reflection in the glass.

 

 You knew what Annie looked like. Everyone did. But seeing her from the inside, wearing this. It hits differently. You feel the sway in your hips. The bounce in your step.

 

 Without looking you can feel Gordon’s eyes on you, clinical and precise. Glancing over at him you notice he doesn’t leer, but his glance tracks your movements just like every other man in Charlton. Just like you remember watching Annie.

 

 He gives a nod toward the bench. “First set. Overhead presses.”

 

 You step into position. The moment you lift the weights, you feel the stretch of your arms pulling your top higher. As you push up and down, you feel your chest noticeably rising and falling with every breath. 

 

 Finishing the set, you stand up and shift on the balls of your feet, becoming aware of the way your back arches naturally. You find yourself naturally smiling at Gordon as he resets the machine. You know how inviting that smile can be.

 

 You should focus on the workout. But part of you wonders if being watched might not be so bad. In this body, it almost feels natural.
 
 
 Whilst you’ve been in the locker room, Gordon has been setting up for the exercise session. He’s placed a bench on the mats in front of the mirror and carefully placed a number of dumbbells to the side. You note the dumbbells are smaller than you’ve used before. Also, they are pink.

 

 “Ready?” Gordon says, making some final adjustments to his preparations. As far as you can tell, he doesn’t react to your change of clothes. His nonchalant attitude quickly puts you at ease a little, in spite of still feeling slightly exposed standing to the side in Jen

passages.name: GymGordonTrainFemaleOutcome
passages.content:
You follow Gordon’s instruction and step into a lunge, but tilt your hips slightly more than needed. As your front leg bends, you feel the shorts ride up just a little further.

 

 Catching your reflection in the mirror, you see a flash of bum cheek as you bend at the waist. You hold the pose longer than necessary and a smile curls on your lips when you see that Gordon has noticed too. 

 

 He calls the next set. You move slowly and deliberately. Leaning forward towards the mirror just enough your reflection shows a view down your top to him. You remember how Annie would lean and she’d smile over her shoulder without trying. You find yourself doing the same, looking back at him, biting your lip.

 

 Gordon's face doesn’t change, but you see his eyes lingering on the woman in the mirror. You’ve had that look on your face looking at Annie before. But the difference this time is the person in the mirror is now you. The young woman, flaunting her body and flashing private areas barely contained by the revealing clothing. How far would you go if he told you to do more?

 

 “Enough,” Gordon says, voice low but firm. “We’re done for today.”

 

 You blink. The moment breaks. You rise from your pose, adjusting the top as subtly as you can. Gordon is already re-racking the weights, his eyes unreadable.

 

 You glance up at the clock on the wall and note that it seems like a short session. You wonder if you made the right decision with him. You nod, and head back to the locker room to get changed.
 
 
 You follow Gordon’s advice and lean backward, but take the opportunity to further push your bum out. Facing back away from him, you can easily picture what Jennifer looks like from behind. Except in this instance, it’s you.

 

 Gordon continues to instruct you in different exercises to try and poses to drop into. Instead of focusing on the exercise though, your mind turns to how you can use it to emphasise your body. With each new stance a singular thought drifts through

passages.name: GymGordonTrainMale
passages.content:
**Casey's Gym**

[image: ./images/Gym.png]

When you return to the gym, you find Gordon adjusting some dumbbells near an exercise mat. "Here", he says. "These should work."

For the next hour Gordon puts you through your paces, encouraging you when you need it, praising you when you push yourself, calling you out when you take it easy. He doesn't belittle you or put you down, even though the dumbbells are much lighter than what you've seen him use.

"And relax," he says to you, after the final set on his plan. You take several deep breaths, short of oxygen. Your body aches, but you have to admit, you feel better.

"Good work.” Gordon says. "Keep coming. You'll see the difference."

You thank him. Even though you feel tired, there's no question that the workout has made you feel stronger.

With your exercise complete, you have a rare moment to talk to Gordon where he isn't preoccupied with work or lifting.

passages.name: GymBoxingClass
passages.content:
**Casey's Gym**

 [image: ./images/Gym.png]

 

 You walk back into the gym. Casey’s finished adjusting the mats and is bouncing lightly on the balls of his feet, rolling his shoulders, shifting his weight, almost dancing. It’s a little uneven, his limp catching now and then, but he moves like someone who remembers at least how it used to feel.

 

 At the sight of you, he stops. “Alright,” he says, taking one last drag from the cigarette. Then, without ceremony, he stubs it out directly on the skirting board. “You ready?”

 

 Before you can answer, he tosses a pair of gloves your way. You fumble slightly catching them, but more from the unexpected speed than the weight. Casey chuckles, but in doing so stumbles a little over his bad leg. He grunts, recovers, and says nothing.

 

 You pull on the gloves slowly, tightening each strap. As you do, your eyes flick toward the window. The boys are still there. Their breath fogs faint patches in the glass. One of them points at Casey and says something you can’t hear, but it makes the others snicker. 

 

 Casey doesn’t look, but his shoulders square just slightly as if he’s trying not to hear what he’s already heard. He steps onto the mat and gestures for you to join him. “Hands up,” he says simply. “Keep your guard tight. Stay light on your feet.”

 

 He adjusts his stance, loose and low, one glove tapping lightly at his chin.

 

 “If you’re ready, we’ll begin.”

 

 Actions

 <>
 
 You step onto the mat, gloves raised. Casey starts circling you, light on his feet despite his leg. His eyes flick across your stance, watching every shift in your balance. Then, without warning, he throws a couple of slow, easy punches toward your gloves.

 

 They land with a soft thud. The technique is still sharp, but there’s no weight behind them. Not anymore.

 

 “Turn that lead foot in,” he mutters. “You’re open on the left. Chin down. Guard tight.”

 

 You adjust, and he nods. He keeps circling, throwing a few more lazy jabs. You

passages.name: GymBalletClass
passages.content:
**Casey's Gym**

 [image: ./images/Gym.png]

 

 You step back into the gym and immediately recognise a few familiar faces gathered near the mirrored wall.
 
 
Florence leans against the barre, her flame red hair pulled into a high ponytail. One foot rests along the barre as she stretches, her gaze sharp.
 
 
 
Annie sits on a bench, tying her ballet slippers with a practiced rhythm. She flicks her blonde hair back through a headband, suddenly glaring when she catches the sight of you.

 
Annie sits on a bench, tying her ballet slippers with a practiced rhythm. She flicks her blonde hair back through a headband without looking up.

 
 
 

 Britney and Stacey stand side by side in front of the mirror. Britney tugs at her leotard with a grimace. “I look frumpy,” she complains, pouting.

 

 You hesitate for a moment as their eyes shift toward you. Then DeBussy steps briskly into your path, clipboard tucked under one arm.

 

 “Ah, . Just in time!” she beams. “Ladies, please line up against the mirror and we’ll begin.”

 

 You take your place among them, the mirror stretching out in front of you. Somewhere in the background, Casey lingers behind the gym counter, disinterested, barely glancing your way. But through the wide gym windows, you see the real audience. A cluster of teenage boys gathered outside, grinning, pointing and waiting.

 

 “Eyes sharp, ladies,” DeBussy calls, smiling as she walks the line. “Now. Let’s get started, shall we?”

 

 **Actions**

 <>
 
 You stand in line with the other girls, your reflection stretching beside theirs in the mirror. DeBussy walks the row slowly, clipboard tucked beneath one arm, a long wooden baton now in her hand.

 

 “Ladies,” she begins, voice crisp. “Ballet is discipline. Ballet is posture. Ballet is presence.” She taps the end of the baton lightly against the floor. “When done properly, you needn’t say a word. You command a room by the way you hold your body.”

 

 She turns toward a small sound system set up by the

**Casey's Gym - Women's Locker Room**

[image: ./images/Gym_locker_room.png]

The women's locker room could do with a clean, but it's bright and spacious. Rows of lockers dominate the space, with a small shower room off to the side.

passages.name: GymLockerRoomChanging
passages.content:
Changing quickly, each layer brings a strange blend of embarrassment and curiosity. The top rides high, the shorts lower still. When you glance in the mirror, what stares back is unmistakably Annie. Flushed cheeks, long legs, bare midriff, the soft contour of a chest framed for visibility.

 

 You should feel exposed. But somehow, you don’t. Instead, you smile in the mirror. There’s a strange thrill that catches in your chest. You’re not sure if this is how Annie feels when she walks into a room. But in her skin and in her clothes, it’s hard not to feel like you’re meant to be looked at.

 

 *Or like some part of you wants to be?*

 

 You breathe in. Gordon will be waiting. Watching. Compared to other men in Charlton, he seemed disinterested in obviously looking at you. But this is Charlton. You’ve learned the hard way that things are never as easy as they seem.

 

 Or do you wish he was more interested in you?

 

 You step toward the exit.

 
 You need to do this, you think, and begin changing your clothes. Putting them on isn’t tricky. You pull the bra over your head and over your breasts. The leggings are surprisingly stretchy and comfortable to wear. You pull and tug them up, ensuring they are free of wrinkles. 

 

 A full-length mirror hangs nearby on the wall. You look at yourself in it, turning to face away and looking at yourself over your shoulder.

 

 The activewear might be comfy to wear, but it looks tight and restrictive, riding up between your buttocks and clinging to your body like paint. The face of Jennifer smiles back at you in the mirror, looking cheeky. You remember that Jennifer looks like this for all her sessions with Gordon. You’re not sure how that makes you feel.

 
 You need to do this, you think, and begin changing your clothes. Each piece fits too snugly. Whether it clings, clasps, or rides high, the end result is the same. When you look in the mirror, there’s no mistaking what you look like.

 

 You might look like Emily, but it

**Charlton Hotel - First Floor**

[image: ./images/Hotel_FirstFloor.png]

The corridor is quiet and dimly lit. Barely a whisper can be heard from downstairs or behind the many guest rooms whose countless doors line the hall.

 
The hotel rooms each have a sturdy lock that keeps guests' activities private. But now that you have the skeleton key, getting access to the rooms will be no problem.

passages.content:
**Charlton Hotel - Kitchen**

[image: ./images/Hotel_Kitchen.png]

The kitchen is all steel and steam. Industrial counters, open burners, and racks lined with mismatched pans. The floor is matte ceramic, dark and practical, worn in places to a shine by years of staff pacing the same frantic routes. It smells alive, like heat and movement and pressure.

 [[Go to reception->HotelLobby]]

passages.name: HotelKitchenMaid
passages.content:
You peel away your clothes with mechanical movements, folding them neatly onto the bench beside the locker. For a moment, you hesitate, just looking at the uniform laid out before you. It’s absurd. Sexy. Impractical. 

 

 You slide into the dress.

 

 It hugs close where it shouldn’t. The fabric is light, clinging to your form with an unsettling accuracy, and the lace feels more decorative than functional. You tug at the hem, but it barely grazes mid-thigh.

 

 The stockings whisper as you pull them up your legs. The heels are a battle of their own. Unsteady, sculpted things that force your hips into a sway you didn’t ask for.

 

 By the time you leave the locker room, the echo of your footsteps down the hallway seems… louder. More noticed. Staff glance as you pass. Guests turn. A man gives a second glance, smiling to himself. You feel exposed. Like decoration.

 

 You reach the kitchen doors and push through.

 
 You slip out of your clothes with practiced ease, folding them with care. The uniform waits for you like a second skin. You no longer hesitate.

 

 The dress slides on smoothly now, its cut familiar, its cling expected. It cinches at the waist and lifts in all the right places. You adjust the hem, but not to tug it lower. Just to smooth it flat.

 

 Stockings stretch up your thighs with a whisper, and the once challenging heels now feel natural. Even flattering. You move differently in them. You know it.

 

 You step out into the hallway. You’re not sure if you’ve stopped noticing the stares… or if you’ve just started accepting them.
 
 
 

 Actions

 <>
 
 
 
 
 

 You don’t even think about it. The second your hands are in the water, something just… clicks. A rhythm. A pull. You scrub the dishes with careful attention, polishing each plate until it gleams. Cutlery gets stacked in perfect alignment. Pans lose their grease like a challenge you’re eager to meet.

 

 You’re not doing this because you want to. Not exactly. You’re doing it because it

[image: ./images/Hotel_Lennox_Office.png]

The room is beyond cramped. An old broom cupboard, judging by the narrow layout and lingering scent of polish. But someone has attempted to make it presentable. A polished wooden desk has been squeezed into one corner, with two iron fold-up chairs placed in front of it.

[image: ./images/Hotel_Lobby.png]

The Charlton Hotel is decorated like a heritage building, all dark oak surfaces and ageing carpets that look like they haven’t been replaced in years.

 <>

*You glance around the lobby, noticing the cleaning cart nearby Andrea. It’s covered in open products and bleaches that are perilously stacked. You wonder what Andrea might be compelled to do if her negligence caused one of the guests to get their clothes stained. At least help them clean-up… in the locker room. It's a gamble, but you think you can chance it. Just once.*

 
*In Andrea’s form, you will be expected to perform your cleaning duties by the hotel staff. You are confident you can sneak out, but being Andrea in the Charlton Hotel gives you unique access to more areas than as yourself.*

 
 
As you enter the hotel lobby, seeing Lennox look up at you. “Miss. Phillips,” he says. “If you are here to take a shift, please go through to the locker room and change.”

passages.name: HotelLobbyLennoxDialogue0
passages.content:
Lennox looks up from the oak podium as you approach him. He gives you a polite smile, but doesn't hide the disdain underneath his attempts to be professional.

 

 "Yes?", he asks. "Can I help you?"

 Lennox looks back at you, waiting impatiently for you to speak.

 

 "Is there anything else?"

passages.name: HotelLobbyAndreaDialogue0
passages.content:
“Hey Andrea,” you say as you approach the maid.

 

 She looks at you and eyes you with a hint of anger and a lot of hurt. She says nothing.

 “Hey Andrea,” you say as you approach the maid.

 

 She looks at you and eyes you with a hint of anger behind her shadowy make-up. "How can I help you?" she says through clenched teeth.
 

 “Hey Andrea,” you say as you approach the maid.

 

 She looks at you and smiles. "Oh hi, Flo," she replies. 

 

 Andrea seems more relaxed than usual around Florence, but her eyes are still alert and nervous.

 She looks at you, slightly nervous. “Oh hi, Annie. I mean, Miss. Clancy.” She looks around, then leans in to whisper. “Is everything to your satisfaction?” 

 

 You give Andrea a look. It's enough to send her eyes wide. "I mean, if there's anything you need, please let me know. If you were a guest here, that is. Which you're not. Obviously. Oh dear."

 

 She leans in closer to you, her lip trembling. "I'm sorry, Annie! Please don't tell Lennox. I don't ever talk about guests with anyone, I swear!”

 

 You give her a polite smile.

 Andrea nervously looks up as you approach. “Hello ma’am. How can I help you?”

 

 You raise an eyebrow. "Ma’am?”

 

 "Lennox says that we should treat all guests professionally,” Andrea mutters to you. “Even people I know.”

 

 "That seems a bit much,” you tell her.

 

 Andrea frowns, looking at you strangely. “I thought you had to do the same at the department store?”

 

 You try to hide a wince. Instead, you smile politely and nod. “Sure.”

 

 Andrea eyes you, her eyes narrowed slightly. “Are you feeling alright, Jen?”

 

 You feel as if you're under a microscope, as you realise that you've underestimated Andrea's relationship with Jen. Now you're arousing her suspicions.

 

 "Just tired,” you reply.
 

 “Hi there,” you say as you approach the maid.

 

 She looks at you, slightly startled. “Yes. Can I help you? Are you looking for someone in particular? Or are you interested in our advert

passages.name: HotelLobbyFirstFloor
passages.content:
You walk up the stairs to the first floor. In your current form as a maid employed by the hotel, no one will question you.

 

 **Actions**

 <>

 You look around the lobby to make sure no one is watching, then quickly leap up the stairs to the first floor.

 

 **Actions**

 <>

 
 You take a step forward towards the stairs, but hear a tutting behind you. You turn to see Lennox eying you quizzically.

 

 “Mr. Thompson has been very specific, Miss. Clancy,” he says with snark. “You are only to go upstairs when accompanying him. To do your business meeting.”
 
 
 You take a step forward towards the stairs, but hear a tutting behind you. You turn to see Lennox eying you quizzically.

 

 “I’m afraid the first floor is reserved for guests,” he says with snark. “And if you are visiting as a guest, please ask them to come down and sign you in. In acceptable attire, if at all possible.”

 
 

 **Actions**

 
 <>
 
 <> 
 
 You step forward on the first stair, only to hear a small cough from behind you. 

 
 
 Looking over your shoulder, you see Andrea. She looks nervous and frightened, but she manages to whisper. “I’m sorry, but you really shouldn’t go up there. It’s for guests only. Please. I’d get into trouble if I let you go up.”

 

 Briefly, you consider if Andrea would have the courage to stop you. But you don’t want to risk her calling Lennox or someone else. You need to reconsider how to get up to the first floor.

 

 **Actions**
 
 
 
 
 <>

 
 <>

 
 
 <>
 
 <>

 **Actions**

 **Actions**

 You step up to the staff changing room and type in the keycode to the locker room, but hear a male voice from behind you before you can open the door.

 

 “Taking a break, Miss. Cinders?” Lennox says to you. 

 

 You shake your head and step away from the door. You’ll need to wait until Lennox isn’t watching you before you can enter the staff room.

 
 
 **Actions**

 You look to the staff room door, but become quickly aware of other people in the lobby. Not wanting to draw attention to yourself, you take a step back. You’ll need to wait until the lobby is empty to avoid suspicion.

passages.name: HotelLobbyAccident
passages.content:
As inconspicuously as you can, you walk towards the cart. Andrea pays you no attention, absorbed in what she is doing.

You thrust your foot underneath the wheel and feel your body twist as your ankle gets caught underneath its base. The cart lurches away and instinctively you reach out to grab something to keep your balance. 

With a creak the cart tips on its side. The world starts to spin and you’re falling backwards, feeling your back slam against the floor before being covered in cleaning fluid spilling from buckets flying through the air.

*That was too successful*, you think, as the cart falls on you, and cleaning fluid soaks through into your clothes.

“Oh my!” you hear Andrea exclaim. She’s squatting down beside you in a heartbeat, lifting the cart off your body. “I’m sorry! I’m so sorry! Are you okay?”

You genuinely check yourself for damage, getting up when you are assured you’re free from injuries. You pull at your clothes, seeped in bleach and fluid. 

Andrea’s eyes flick around, filled with terror. She gives your ruined attire a look, then grimaces. “Please don’t say anything!”

"It's alright," you tell her. “Accidents happen. I would like to get out of these clothes, though.”

She bites her lip, conflict visible on her face. “4-9-2-1,” she says. “That’s the locker room code. Wait there for me, I’ll clean up here and come find you something to wear.”

Andrea turns back to the cart and the mess, hurriedly tidying up.

**Actions**

<>

passages.name: HotelLobbyWork
passages.content:
You enter the lobby and glance at the duty board near the podium, where Lennox’s scratchy handwriting has been scrawled beneath the staff roster. One note stands out:

 

 “Lunchtime: Bar support (Andrea). Staff rotation short again. Smile and serve.” 
 

 You sigh. You’re supposed to be a maid, not bar staff. But the Charlton Hotel seems to run permanently understaffed. And you’re learning quickly that saying no isn’t really part of the culture here.
 
 

 **Actions**

 <>
 
 
 The lobby rota still reads the same:

 

 "Lunchtime: Bar support (Andrea). Smile and serve."
 

 No one’s fixed the staffing issue. And no one’s likely to. By now, you’ve accepted that being a maid at the Charlton Hotel comes with an elastic job description. One that includes bar duty whenever the schedule demands it.

 

 **Actions**

 <>
 
 
 
 
 
 You check the schedule on the lobby board. Another line in Lennox’s jagged handwriting:

 

 "Afternoon – Lobby carpet detail (Andrea). Bucket + cloth. Back straight. Smile." 
 

 You sigh. Loudly. But there’s no one to hear it or care. A proper hotel might have a machine for this kind of work. But the Charlton Hotel is anything but modern. 

 

 You find the battered bucket tucked behind the reception desk, fill it with water and soap, and grab a fraying cloth. Your knees hit the ground, and the work begins.

 

 The carpet is an endless expanse of intricate patterns and ancient stains. No matter how hard you scrub, the marks remain. Like memories burned into fabric. You try to focus on the rhythm of the cloth, the swish of water, but your uniform clings tighter with each motion. Riding up the more you lean in.

 

 You catch a few glances from passing guests. Some watch too long. Others pretend not to, but their eyes linger. Bent forward, on all fours, your skirt creeping higher… it’s hard not to imagine what they’re imagining.

 

 And then the lobby doors open.

 

 Two figures walk in. One, you recognize. Mr. Sermon, the hotel owner. Tall.

passages.name: HotelLobbyWorkResponses
passages.content:
**Charlton Hotel - Restaurant**

 [image: ./images/Hotel_Restaurant.png]

 

 
 
 By the time you step behind the polished mahogany counter, customers are already trickling in. You’re midway through aligning cocktail napkins when two familiar figures stroll through the arch.

 

 Clive, sun-kissed and effortlessly charming, heads straight to the bar with a grin. Gordon, silent and stoic, falls into step beside him, like a man doing his duty.

 

 "Afternoon, darlin’,” Clive says easily, leaning on the counter like it’s made for him. “They’ve got you working the bar again? Underappreciated, that’s what you are. Though I’m not about to complain, seeing your face across from me.”

 

 You offer a polite smile. “What can I get you?”

 

 “Two pints of the old reliable,” Clive replies. “Been a busy mornin’.”

 

 Gordon grunts. “Water.”

 

 Clive rolls his eyes. “He’s in monk mode again. Got a gym session or something. I don’t know how he does it. No joy, this one.”

 

 “Focus,” Gordon says.

 

 "Yeah, yeah.” Clive waves a hand, then glances at you with a smirk. “You believe that? All work, no play. Fella’s gonna forget how to smile.”

 

 **Actions**

 <>

 
 You walk into the restaurant and slide behind the counter, heels already aching, just as Clive and Gordon appear again. You barely have time to adjust the glasses before Clive’s grin flashes across the room.

 

 “There she is,” he drawls. “Charlton’s very own reason to drink at lunch.”

 

 Gordon nods silently and takes his usual seat.

 

 “Two of the usual?” you ask.

 

 “‘Course,” Clive says, flashing you a wink. “Though I don’t know why I even bother ordering for him. Gordo’s in a committed relationship with water and protein shakes.”

 

 “Discipline,” Gordon mutters, without looking up.

 

 Clive groans theatrically. “Says it like a virtue.”

 

 You return with their drinks - Clive's pint, Gordon's water - sliding them across the bar with practiced ease.

 

 Clive takes his with a pleased nod. “You a

passages.name: HotelLobbyWorkEvents
passages.content:
As you make your way through the hotel, you totter around a corner in your heels as you rush to get back to the lobby, and barge straight into Lennox. Try as you might, this time you lose your balance on your stilettos and fall to the floor.

 

 “Miss. Cinders!“, he chides you, towering over your crumpled form. “Have some decency. I’m sure the guests don’t want to see your under-garments.”

 

 He doesn’t offer to help you up. Like a newborn deer, you stagger to your feet and brush down your clothing, pulling at your skirt, wishing it was longer.

 

 “I have a specific ask of you,” he murmurs. “The rooms need cleaning. Take my key. Be aware that some guests will still be in their rooms. I expect you not to disturb them.”

 

 He hands you a key, eyes your clothing, then turns and walks away.
 
 

 **Actions**

 <>

passages.content:
**Hotel Charlton - Staff Locker Room**

[image: ./images/Hotel_Locker_Room.png]

Even for Charlton, the locker room is in bad need of maintenance. The carpets are stained and the mirror on the wall is chipped and cracked. A lone sofa is pushed against the only wall without lockers, its covering ripped in many places exposing the foam underneath.

 

 
 
 

 Andrea lies peacefully asleep on the sofa where you left her, her chest rising and falling as she breathes shallowly.

 

 **Actions**

passages.name: SleepForgetAndrea2
passages.content:
“Fall asleep,” you spit out, “and let your memories drift as you slumber.”

With little dignity, Andrea falls straight onto the sofa, passing out. You instinctively move towards her to check if she’s okay, but almost fall yourself as you become aware you are now wearing five inch stiletto heels.

Regaining your balance, you squat down beside her. You feel yourself keep your stocking-clad legs squeezed together, now even more aware of how short the French Maid’s skirt actually is.

She breathes normally. Captive of a deep, deep sleep.

Standing again, you take a moment to adjust to the heels. You glance over at the smeared mirror on the wall and see your reflection. A young woman, dressed in a skimpy French Maid costume, her innocence contrasting distinctly with the black eyeshadow and dark red lipstick that she wears.

You notice how petite you now are. The room feels like it’s grown. A shudder runs through your body, as you think about how you look, how you are dressed, and how it makes you feel.

Thoughts drift towards conflict and confrontation. You know that in this form, you would struggle to *assert yourself* over even the gentlest of people.

**Actions**

<>

<>

passages.content:
You carefully search Andrea for her skeleton key, conscious of her skimpy outfit and her barely concealed body. 

 
 There’s not many places she can hide a key, and quickly you find it hanging around a necklace she wears.

 

 You unclip the necklace and take it from her, leaving her still sleeping peacefully.

 

 **Actions**

 <> 
 
 Unfortunately, it doesn't look like Andrea has the key on her at the moment. You will need to search elsewhere to gain access to the hotel rooms.

 

 **Actions**

 <>

passages.name: CauseAccident
passages.content:
You step forward and lean into the locker, plucking the passport from the shelf and grabbing it firmly in hand. 

 

 In spite of Andrea’s obvious attempts to avoid looking at you, your brazen theft does not go unnoticed. She spins around, looking between your face and the passport in your hand.

 

 “Give that back!” she demands. There is more than a little desperation in her voice. 

 

 **Actions**

 <>
 
 <>

 
 
 <>
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Despite the opportunity of taking her passport, you decide against it. You instead stand back, waiting for her to find something for you to wear.

 

 **Actions**

 <>
 
 
 After a long search, she pulls out a pair of black leggings and a strappy sports crop top with a scooped neckline that you know will expose the tops of your breasts. You wonder for a moment about this Amber who once owned the clothing.

 
 
 “That’s the best I could find,” she tells you. “I hope that’s okay!”

 

 You take the clothes from her and change. The clothes are tight. You feel the fabric of the leggings hugging your bum. When you put on the crop-top, you look down and confirm your fears that the activewear flaunts your assets without shame.

 

 Andrea looks at you expectantly. You offer her a pleasant smile. “Thank you,” she says. “For not making a scene.”

 

 You notice her eyes are moist, holding back tears. A single streak of black eyeshadow runs down her cheek with a tear. She sniffles, and wipes it away with her hand.

 

 “Our secret,” you tell her. “Come on, you should get back before people notice.”

 

 She nods. For the first time that you’ve noticed, her nervousness dissipates a little. “Thank you,” she tells you again.

 
 

 After a long search, she pulls out a pair of tweed trousers and a white shirt, stained with kitchen grease. She holds them out to you, still facing the locker. “That’s the best I could find,” she tells you. “I hope that’s okay!”

 

 You take the clothes from her and change. The clothes are big on you and you have t

passages.name: FlorenceEncounter
passages.content:
You quickly reach out and swipe the passport from Andrea's hand. Not expecting it, she loses grip of it before she realises what's going on.

 

 “Give that back!” she demands. There is more than a little desperation in her voice. 
 
 

 **Actions**

 <>
 
 <>

 
 
 <>
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 You glance down at the passport. In spite of its obvious value to you, a part of you doesn't want to upset Andrea.

 

 "It's fine," you tell her. "Just wanted to see if your photo had a hologram in the material. Mine doesn't."

 

 "You've got a passport?" Andrea exclaims, her eyes wide as a grin creeps up. "Are you thinking of travelling too?"

 

 You inwardly grimace at the impact of a throw-away lie. You shrug.

 

 "We could go travelling together," Andrea whispers to you.

 

 "Maybe," you say. "We should get back to the lobby. So you don't get in trouble."

 

 Andrea nods, but the smile doesn't leave her face. You wonder what her next conversation with the real Florence will be like...

 

 **Actions**

 <>
 
 “I’m sorry,” you say. “I just wanted to see your picture.”

 

 She quickly takes her passport from your hand and tosses it back in her locker, closing the door and locking it. 

 

 “Sorry,” Andrea says after a moment’s silence. “It’s just very important to me.”

 

 She looks up at you from underneath her eyebrows and you see her eyes are full of tears. 

 

 **Actions**

 <>

 “How about I just hold on to this,” you tell her. “Foreign countries are dangerous places, anyway.”

 

 Andrea’s eyes well-up with tears. “You… you… please!” 

 

 You feel yourself grinning, aware of the power you hold in your hand. It’s intoxicating.

 

 “No,” you say after a moment. “I don’t think so."

 

 "But Flo!" she stammers. "I thought we were friends!"

 

 You give her a look, seeing the visible sense of betrayal on her face. Out of all the people she expected to take advantage of her in Charlton, you imagine Florence wasn't one of them.

 <>

passages.name: FlorenceLure
passages.content:
"I managed to get this," you tell her, holding out her passport.

 

 She glances at it, frowning. "What's that?"

 

 "It's your passport," you reply. Her eyes look confused.

 

 "Flo, why would I need a passport?" she asks you. "Foreign countries are dangerous."

 

 She shakes her head. "I've got to get back. Lennox will kill me if he finds us in here."

 

 She walks back over to the door, with almost no interest in the passport you hold in your hand.

 

 **Actions**

 <> 
 
 
 
 “I’m sorry,” you say. “I just wanted to see your picture. I don’t want anything for it at all.”

 

 She looks at you cautiously, as you hold out the passport. When you don’t react, she takes a quick step forward and takes it out of your hand. She flicks through it to check nothing has been tampered with, then looks up at you confused.

 

 “Thank you,” she whispers, just on the side of politeness. Andrea then walks over to her locker and locks her passport away. 

 

 Silence hangs in the air between the two of you. The young woman still anticipating a demand. 

 

 “We should go,” you tell her. Her eyes stare at you, incredulous.

passages.name: PCLure
passages.content:
“It's okay,” you say. “I don’t want anything for it at all.”

 
 “I’m sorry,” you say. “I just wanted to see your picture. I don’t want anything for it at all.”

 
 

 She looks at you cautiously, as you hold out the passport. When you don’t react, she takes a quick step forward and takes it out of your hand. She flicks through it to check nothing has been tampered with, then looks up at you confused.

 

 “Thank you,” she whispers, just on the side of politeness. Andrea then walks over to her locker and locks her passport away. 

 

 Silence hangs in the air between the two of you. The young woman still anticipating a demand. 

 

 “We should go,” you tell her. Her eyes stare at you, incredulous. 

 

 You walk over to the door and hold it open for her. After only the slightest hesitation, she walks quickly past you and back into the lobby.

 <>

 “I’ve changed my mind,” you tell her flatly. “I’ll think I’ll just hold on to it for a while.”

 

 She looks at you with pleading eyes. Her lips purse. “Do you want me to beg?” she asks quietly. “Beg for you to give it back?”

 

 You stare back at the young woman. Her eyes are full of barely concealed hate. 

 

 You shake your head just a little. “We should go,” you tell her. “You wouldn’t want to get in trouble.”

 

 You walk back to the door and open it. She gives you a look of utter disgust, then quickly leaves the locker room.

 Seconds later, her eyes hazily open. She looks up at you with her large dark eyes.

passages.name: PCIntimidate
passages.content:
Andrea gives you a confused look. "Why?"

 

 "I just need to check something," you tell her.

 

 She eyes you carefully, but turns to a locker and unlocks it with a key. A ladies T-shirt and jeans are hung on a rail inside, along with a purse. You note a number of pictures of European cities are taped to the inside of the door.

 

 Andrea riffles around inside the locker for a moment, then turns with a passport in-hand. She sidles up to you, flicking through the passport and opening it up to her picture and details. "Is something wrong with it?" she asks, nervously.
 
 

 **Actions**

 <>

 <> 
 
 
 You quickly reach out and swipe the passport from Andrea's hand. Not expecting it, she loses grip of it before she realises what's going on.

 

 “Give that back!” she demands. There is more than a little desperation in her voice.

 <>

 
 
 <>
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 You glance down at the passport. In spite of its obvious value to you, a part of you doesn't want to upset Andrea.

 

 "It's fine," you tell her. "Just wanted to see if your photo had a hologram in the material. Mine doesn't."

 

 "You've got a passport?" Andrea asks, a sparkle appearing in her eye momentarily. "I guess you would, after living in the city for most of your life."

 

 You nod. "I've been to a few places."

 

 "I'd love to hear about them!" Andrea exclaims.

 

 "Maybe," you say. "We should grab a coffee one evening. I'll tell you all about them."
 

 
 Andrea smiles a little. You see a slight blush appear in her cheeks underneath her make-up. "I'd like that," she says quietly. 

 
 Andrea's eyebrows narrow just slightly, wary. She offers you a timid and polite smile. "Maybe."

 
 "We should get back to the lobby. So you don't get in trouble," you tell her.

 

 Andrea nods.

 Andrea’s eyes well-up with tears. “You… you… please!”

passages.name: HotelLockerRoomAndreaOutsideEvent
passages.content:
Sermon doesn’t wait to see if you’ll follow - he simply turns and starts walking, expecting you to keep pace behind him.

 

 Your heels click sharply against the pavement as you struggle to keep up, each step in the impossibly high stilettos making your balance feel precarious at best.

 

 People stare as you pass - shopkeepers, townsfolk, hotel guests lingering by the entrance. Their gazes linger just a second too long, their expressions ranging from amusement to quiet judgment.

 

 Sermon says nothing, but his jaw tightens visibly. By the time you reach the hotel entrance, he pushes the door open without a word and quickly escorts you into the locker room.

 

 You almost fall through the entrance, as he pushes you gently from behind. But he doesn’t follow. Instead, he stays by the door, hands folded neatly behind his back, his expression impassive but sharp.

 

 “Wait here,” he states, his voice cold and professional, as if he were dealing with a simple inconvenience rather than reprimanding a member of his staff.

 

 You open your mouth - to explain, to protest, to say something - but his expression doesn’t invite discussion. So you stand there, the weight of the hotel uniform, the tiny skirt, the towering heels suddenly feeling heavier than before.

 

 Sermon checks his watch, then exhales slowly. “Lennox will deal with you shortly.”

 

 And with that, he turns sharply on his heel, stepping out of the room.

 

 The door lock clicks shut behind him, leaving you alone in the empty, sterile quiet of the locker room.

 <>
 
 **Hotel Charlton - Staff Locker Room**

 [image: ./images/Hotel_Locker_Room.png]

 

 The air is still, thick with anticipation, the faint sounds of the hotel distant, muffled behind layers of walls and doors.

 

 Then, the door swings open and Lennox steps inside. His usual air of sarcasm and disdain is present, but as his eyes flick down to your uniform, a slow, knowing smirk curls across his lips.

 

 “Oh dear, Mis

passages.name: HotelRestaurantDialogueDan0
passages.content:
Dan leans back in his chair, rolling his shoulders like he’s already bored. He doesn’t make conversation right away, letting the silence sit, watching you with that lazy smirk. His fingers tap idly against the table.

 

 “You know, I don’t usually take the time to mentor people,” he says after a beat, like this lunch is some grand favor. “But I figure you could use a few pointers. Charlton’s not the kind of place where you get ahead by keeping your head down.”

 

 His gaze flickers toward the other tables, taking in the room, or maybe making sure people are taking him in.
 
 

 You wonder how to sway the conversation to something at least... useful.

 Andrea arrives, placing Dan’s steak pie in front of him before setting your salad down with a small, apologetic smile. “Here you go. Let me know if you need anything else.”

 

 Dan barely acknowledges her, already reaching for his fork. “See, that’s good service,” he remarks as she walks away, not even lowering his voice. “Not just standing around waiting to be noticed. Knows her place.”

 

 He shovels a bite of pie into his mouth, speaking around it. “That’s the trick to getting anywhere. Knowing where you fit in, playing the game.” He gestures vaguely at you with his fork. “Figure that out, and work might start going a little easier for you.”

 

 The man has a tendency to steer the conversation regardless of what you say, but you try again to get something of value out of him.

 You pick at your salad while Dan tears through his meal, completely at ease. Between mouthfuls, he talks. Mostly about himself. His time as a tennis coach, the “right kind” of people he rubs elbows with, how the world works when you’re smart enough to play the game.

 

 “Charlton’s full of opportunities,” he says, chewing lazily. “But only if you know how to handle them. People think hard work gets you ahead. Nah. It’s about who notices you.”

 

 He points his fork at you like he’s letting you in on some great secret. “That’s why I wan

passages.name: HotelRestaurantAndreaOutside
passages.content:
You swallow hard, your voice coming out quieter than you want it to.

 

 “I… I apologize,” you say, forcing the words out, your gaze locked on the floor. “For wearing my uniform outside the hotel. It was improper.”

 

 “Louder,” Lennox urges, a cruel smirk playing at his lips.

 

 You feel heat rise to your cheeks, shame curling in your chest as you force yourself to repeat it, this time louder, clearer, for everyone to hear.

 

 “I apologize for disgracing the hotel’s uniform. It won’t happen again.”

 

 “And don’t you feel that your superiors are generous in providing you such a lovely uniform free of charge?”

 

 You catch his eyeline. Waiting on you. “Yes,” you meekly reply.

 

 He raises an eyebrow. He expects more.

 

 “Yes sir,” you say. “Thank you for providing me such a lovely uniform.”

 

 “Do you like wearing it?” he asks, smiling thinly.

 

 “Yes,” you mumble. “I love wearing it. It’s my pride and joy.” 

 

 Silence lingers for a moment, before you hear a few muffled chuckles from some of the guests. The humiliation sinks deeper.

 

 Lennox tilts his head approvingly, then gives your shoulder a light pat, like you’re some child who just learned their lesson.

 

 “There now,” he says, grinning down at you. “Was that so hard?”

 

 His hand lingers just a moment too long before he finally steps away, turning his back to you.

 

 “Back to work,” he says, as if none of this ever happened.

 

 People return to their conversations, but you can still feel their eyes lingering on you, their whispers brushing against your skin like needles.

 

 Your stomach churns as you quickly duck your head, slipping away from the center of attention.

 

 Your assertiveness has taken a severe hit. And worse - you know you won’t stand up for yourself again anytime soon.

 You stiffen, heat rising to your cheeks as all eyes in the restaurant remain fixed on you.

 

 The weight of their expectation, their judgment, presses down like a suffocating hand, but somet

passages.name: HotelRoom101
passages.content:
**Charlton Hotel - Room 101**

[image: ./images/Hotel_Room101.png]

A small room with a double bed and built in wardrobes that perpetually creak as a draft blows through the rattling window pane.

 
 
 
 
You enter the room to see a muscular and stocky man looking at photos pinned to the wall. From a cursory glance, you see that the photos are of jocks from Charlton Upper School, playing football. 

 

 “Ah,” he says, seeing you. You can’t help but notice a small grin on his face. “I was wondering if this place actually cleaned the rooms. Go ahead.”

 

 He returns to looking at the photos, but you notice that his eyes drift to you as you linger.

passages.name: HotelRoom101Cleaning
passages.content:
You move to the bed and begin to make it. You aren’t sure what you are doing, but based on his lack of disapproval, it’s what he expects. You notice that there are photos scattered on the floor as well.

 

 “Are these photos of the school football team?” you ask, as you fold the sheets.

 

 “Yup,” he replies. “I’m in town to see if there’s any diamonds in the rough.” You glance behind you and see his eyes lingering on your legs. “Do you mind picking them up off the floor and putting them on the table? Don’t want you to tread on them.”

 

 You look at him watching you, grinning. What you’re dressed like is not lost on you.

 

 **Actions**

 <>

 <>

 You’re not sure why, but there’s something about him watching you clean that gives you butterflies in your stomach. 

 

 You offer him a coy smile, then bend over at your waist to pick up the photos. You feel your skirt rising up, exposing your stocking-clad bum to his view.

 

 “So you’re a talent scout?” you ask him, picking up the photos. You don’t look back, but you grin, knowing where he must be looking.

 

 “Yup,” you hear him say. “Used to be a football player myself.”

 

 You dare to look over your shoulder, still bent over, seeing his eyes looking directly at your arse. “I can tell,” you murmur. He grins.

 

 Picking up the last of the photos, you place them on the bed. You feel a rush of endorphins flow through your body. What am I doing? you think. Did that just turn me on?

 

 You turn to face him. His eyes wander over your body before staring back into yours. "So you're looking for new talent?"

 

 He nods. "Now I spend my evenings looking over photos of skinny kids trying to impress the camera." He glances at your legs again. "Maybe you could clean my room again and keep me company?"

 

 You feel your cheeks redden. He grins.

 

 **Actions**

 
 <>

 <>

passages.name: HotelRoom102
passages.content:
**Charlton Hotel - Room 102**

[image: ./images/Hotel_Room102.png]

A sparsely decorated double room, with aging furniture and dated wallpaper.

 
 
 You open the door to the room and are immediately struck by the sounds of grunting and panting. Before you can stop yourself, you have taken a step inside and are greeted by a squeal.

 

 Your mouth drops as you identify the occupants. Annie is bent over the end of the bed, her skirt pulled around her waist and her breasts hanging free in the breeze. Behind her is Mr. Thompson, trousers around his ankles, his gut hanging over her bum.
 
 

 Upon seeing you, Thompson looks furious. “Get the fuck out of here!” he yells. “We don’t need housekeeping!”

 

 You mutter some apologies and hurriedly back away to the door. You catch Annie’s face as you leave. She’s clearly embarrassed, but even so doesn’t look like she was in the throes of ecstasy anyway.
